name: shell
description: Execute a shell command in the workspace repository. Use this for running builds, tests, installing dependencies, searching files, and any other command-line operations. Commands run in /workspace/repo with sh -c.

name: file_read
description: Read the contents of a file or list a directory. Paths are relative to the workspace repository root (/workspace/repo).

path.description: Relative path to the file or directory

name: file_write
description: Write content to a file in the workspace repository. Creates parent directories if needed. Paths are relative to the workspace repository root (/workspace/repo).

content.description: The content to write to the file

name: git
description: Execute a git command in the workspace repository. Use for status, diff, log, add, commit, push, branch operations, etc.

args.description: Additional arguments for the git subcommand

name: memory_store
description: Store a piece of knowledge in persistent memory (mem9). Use this to remember important decisions, patterns, bugs found, architecture notes, or anything that should persist across iterations.

content.description: The content to store in memory

name: memory_search
description: Search persistent memory (mem9) for relevant past knowledge. Use this at the start of work to recall context, decisions, and patterns from previous iterations.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)
